title: CommuteBuddy BLE Message Schema
description: Documents the BLE message payloads exchanged between the Android companion app and the Garmin Connect IQ watch app via the Connect IQ Mobile SDK.

phoneToWatch.description: Message sent from Android app to Garmin watch via ConnectIQ.sendMessage()

commuteStatus.description: Commute decision payload — a structured JSON object produced by Gemini Flash decision-engine classification of raw MTA alert text.

action.description: Decision tier: NORMAL = good service, MINOR_DELAYS = minor delays tolerable, REROUTE = take alternate route, STAY_HOME = major disruption

summary.example: N/W suspended between Times Sq and Queensboro Plaza

affected_routes.description: Comma-separated list of affected route(s). May be empty string for NORMAL action.

reroute_hint.description: Optional alternate route suggestion. Present only when action is REROUTE.
reroute_hint.example: Take R to 34 St then transfer to 4/5

timestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp (seconds) of when this status was generated

notes: Sent as a Dictionary via ConnectIQ.sendMessage() — the Connect IQ SDK handles serialization.
On the watch side, received as a Dictionary in the Communications.registerForPhoneAppMessages() callback.
Total serialized size must stay well under the 1KB BLE payload limit.
The Android app validates and deserializes the Gemini Flash cloud API output into a Kotlin data class before sending.
If AI output fails validation, the phone falls back to action=NORMAL with summary describing the error.
reroute_hint is omitted entirely (not null) when action is not REROUTE.
Garmin Glance memory budget is approximately 32KB total; BLE message payloads must stay under 1KB.
Never send protobuf or large binary blobs — parse heavy data on Android and send only the result.
Never parse JSON on the watch — the Android app sends a pre-structured Dictionary via the Connect IQ SDK.
AI decision engine uses Gemini Flash cloud API; the Android app calls the API over the network.
